# BizUp Pay — Full LLM Reference

> One TypeScript SDK for every Israeli payment provider. Write payment code once, swap providers by config.

Docs: https://pay.bizup.dev
GitHub: https://github.com/bizup-dev/bizup-pay
Quick guide: https://pay.bizup.dev/llms.txt

---

## Architecture

Three-layer design: core + provider adapter + browser client.

```
Your Server                          Browser
┌──────────────────────────┐        ┌──────────────────────┐
│ @bizup-pay/core          │        │ @bizup-pay/client    │
│   createProvider()       │        │   BizupPay.mount()   │
│   types, errors, factory │        │   iframe/modal/redir │
├──────────────────────────┤        └──────────────────────┘
│ @bizup-pay/cardcom       │
│ @bizup-pay/morning       │  ←── pick one (or many)
│ @bizup-pay/icount        │
│ @bizup-pay/grow          │
└──────────────────────────┘
```

Every provider adapter implements the same `BizupProvider` interface. Your application code only talks to the interface — never to provider APIs directly.

---

## Install

```bash
# Server: core + one or more providers
npm install @bizup-pay/core @bizup-pay/cardcom

# Browser (optional): payment UI mounting
npm install @bizup-pay/client

# Testing (optional): local mock servers
npm install --save-dev @bizup-pay/mock-server
```

---

## Provider Configuration

### Cardcom

```typescript
import { createProvider } from '@bizup-pay/core'
import '@bizup-pay/cardcom'

const provider = createProvider('cardcom', {
  terminalNumber: 1000,           // required — your Cardcom terminal number
  apiName: 'your_api_name',       // required
  apiPassword: 'your_api_pass',   // optional, depends on terminal config
})
```

### Morning (Green Invoice)

```typescript
import '@bizup-pay/morning'

const provider = createProvider('morning', {
  apiKey: 'your_api_key',         // required
  apiSecret: 'your_api_secret',   // required
  sandbox: true,                  // optional — use sandbox environment
})
```

### iCount

```typescript
import '@bizup-pay/icount'

const provider = createProvider('icount', {
  companyId: 'your_company_id',   // required
  user: 'api_user',               // required
  pass: 'api_pass',               // required
})
```

### Grow.il (Meshulam)

```typescript
import '@bizup-pay/grow'

const provider = createProvider('grow', {
  pageCode: 'your_page_code',     // required
  apiKey: 'your_api_key',         // required
})
```

---

## Provider Interface

Every provider implements these 4 methods:

```typescript
interface BizupProvider {
  readonly name: ProviderName  // 'morning' | 'cardcom' | 'icount' | 'grow'

  createSession(params: CreateSessionParams): Promise<BizupPaymentSession>
  getTransaction(id: string): Promise<BizupTransaction>
  refund(params: RefundParams): Promise<BizupRefund>
  parseWebhook(body: unknown, headers?: Record<string, string>): Promise<BizupWebhookEvent>
}
```

---

## Types

### CreateSessionParams (input to createSession)

```typescript
{
  amount: number                    // required — payment amount
  currency?: string                 // default: 'ILS'
  description: string               // required — shown to customer
  customer?: {
    name?: string
    email?: string
    phone?: string
  }
  successUrl: string                // required — redirect after successful payment
  failureUrl?: string               // redirect after failed payment
  cancelUrl?: string                // redirect if customer cancels
  webhookUrl: string                // required — server endpoint for payment notifications
  metadata?: Record<string, string> // custom key-value pairs passed through
  installments?: {
    min?: number
    max?: number
  }
  language?: string                 // 'he' | 'en' | 'ar'
  recurring?: {
    interval: 'month' | 'year'
    count?: number
  }
}
```

### BizupPaymentSession (output of createSession)

```typescript
{
  id: string                        // session ID
  provider: ProviderName
  amount: number
  currency: string
  description: string
  pageUrl: string                   // URL to redirect customer to — or mount with client SDK
  successUrl: string
  failureUrl?: string
  cancelUrl?: string
  webhookUrl: string
  metadata?: Record<string, string>
  status: 'pending' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'cancelled'
  operation?: string                // provider-specific operation ID
  expiresAt?: Date
}
```

### BizupTransaction (output of getTransaction and parseWebhook)

```typescript
{
  id: string                        // BizUp-normalized transaction ID
  providerTransactionId: string     // raw provider transaction ID
  provider: ProviderName
  amount: number
  currency: string
  status: TransactionStatus         // 'pending' | 'completed' | 'failed' | 'cancelled' | 'refunded'
  paymentMethod: PaymentMethod      // 'credit_card' | 'bit' | 'apple_pay' | 'google_pay' | 'bank_transfer' | 'paypal'
  cardBrand?: CardBrand             // 'visa' | 'mastercard' | 'amex' | 'isracard' | 'diners' | 'discover' | 'jcb'
  cardLastFour?: string
  installments?: number
  documentUrl?: string              // link to invoice/receipt PDF
  customer?: {
    name?: string
    email?: string
    phone?: string
  }
  createdAt: Date

  // Provider-specific extras (typed per provider)
  morning?: MorningExtras
  cardcom?: CardcomExtras
  icount?: ICountExtras
  grow?: GrowExtras

  raw: unknown                      // full raw provider response — escape hatch
}
```

### BizupWebhookEvent (output of parseWebhook)

```typescript
{
  type: 'payment.completed' | 'payment.failed' | 'payment.cancelled'
  transaction: BizupTransaction
  timestamp: Date
}
```

### BizupRefund (output of refund)

```typescript
{
  id: string
  transactionId: string
  amount: number
  status: 'pending' | 'completed' | 'failed'
  createdAt: Date
}
```

### RefundParams (input to refund)

```typescript
{
  transactionId: string             // required
  amount?: number                   // optional — partial refund amount (defaults to full)
}
```

---

## Error Handling

All errors are instances of `BizupPayError`:

```typescript
import { BizupPayError } from '@bizup-pay/core'

try {
  const session = await provider.createSession(params)
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof BizupPayError) {
    err.code           // error category — see below
    err.provider       // which provider threw
    err.message        // human-readable description
    err.providerError  // raw error from provider API (for debugging)
  }
}
```

### Error Codes

| Code | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `AUTHENTICATION_ERROR` | Invalid API credentials |
| `VALIDATION_ERROR` | Invalid parameters (missing fields, bad values) |
| `PROVIDER_ERROR` | Provider API returned an error |
| `NETWORK_ERROR` | Could not reach provider API |
| `NOT_FOUND` | Transaction or resource not found |

---

## Payment Methods by Provider

| Method | Morning | Cardcom | iCount | Grow |
|--------|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| Credit Card | yes | yes | yes | yes |
| Bit | yes | yes | yes | yes |
| Apple Pay | yes | yes | — | yes |
| Google Pay | yes | yes | — | yes |
| PayPal | yes | yes | yes | — |
| Bank Transfer | yes | — | — | yes |

---

## Browser Client SDK

### Install

```bash
npm install @bizup-pay/client
```

Or via CDN:

```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@bizup-pay/client/dist/bizup-pay.min.js"></script>
```

### Usage

```typescript
import { BizupPay } from '@bizup-pay/client'

const bizupPay = new BizupPay()

// Mount in a container (iframe mode)
bizupPay.mount(session, document.getElementById('payment-container'), {
  onSuccess: (data) => { /* payment succeeded */ },
  onFailure: (error) => { /* payment failed */ },
  onCancel: () => { /* customer cancelled */ },
})
```

### Mounting Modes

| Mode | Behavior |
|------|----------|
| `iframe` | Embeds payment page in a container element |
| `modal` | Opens payment page in an overlay modal |
| `redirect` | Navigates browser to provider's payment page |

---

## Provider API Mapping

How BizUp Pay methods map to provider APIs:

| BizUp Method | Morning | Cardcom | iCount | Grow |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `createSession` | `POST /payments/form` | `POST /LowProfile/Create` | `POST /paypage/generate_sale` | `POST /createPaymentProcess` |
| `getTransaction` | `GET /documents/{id}` | `POST /Transactions/GetTransactionInfoById` | `POST /doc/info` | `POST /getPaymentProcessInfo` |
| `refund` | `POST /documents` (credit note) | `POST /Transactions/RefundByTransactionId` | `POST /doc/cancel` + `refund_cc` | `POST /cancelTransaction` |
| `parseWebhook` | Document payload | LowProfile webhook | IPN payload | Webhook payload |
| Auth | Bearer token (key:secret) | Per-request body params | SID (cached 19min) | API key in body |

---

## Testing with Mock Server

```bash
npm install --save-dev @bizup-pay/mock-server
```

### Programmatic (for unit/integration tests)

```typescript
import { createMockServer } from '@bizup-pay/mock-server'

const mock = createMockServer({ autoComplete: true })
await mock.start()

// Configure provider to point at mock server
const provider = createProvider('cardcom', {
  terminalNumber: 1000,
  apiName: 'test',
  // baseUrl will point to localhost mock
})

// ... run tests ...

mock.reset()       // clear state between tests
await mock.stop()
```

### Standalone (for development)

```bash
npx bizup-mock-server
# Starts mock servers on ports 4100 (Morning), 4200 (Cardcom), 4300 (iCount)
```

Mock servers provide:
- Payment HTML pages with success/fail buttons
- Webhook delivery to your webhookUrl
- postMessage support for iframe/modal testing
- `autoComplete` mode for fully automated test flows
- Transaction storage for getTransaction() testing

---

## Complete Integration Example

### Next.js API Route (server)

```typescript
// app/api/checkout/route.ts
import { createProvider } from '@bizup-pay/core'
import '@bizup-pay/cardcom'

const provider = createProvider('cardcom', {
  terminalNumber: Number(process.env.CARDCOM_TERMINAL),
  apiName: process.env.CARDCOM_API_NAME!,
  apiPassword: process.env.CARDCOM_API_PASSWORD!,
})

export async function POST(req: Request) {
  const { amount, description } = await req.json()

  const session = await provider.createSession({
    amount,
    description,
    currency: 'ILS',
    successUrl: `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_URL}/success`,
    webhookUrl: `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_URL}/api/webhook`,
  })

  return Response.json({ pageUrl: session.pageUrl, sessionId: session.id })
}
```

### Webhook Handler (server)

```typescript
// app/api/webhook/route.ts
export async function POST(req: Request) {
  const body = await req.json()
  const event = await provider.parseWebhook(body)

  if (event.type === 'payment.completed') {
    const tx = event.transaction
    // tx.id, tx.amount, tx.status, tx.paymentMethod, tx.cardLastFour, tx.documentUrl
    await db.orders.update({ transactionId: tx.id, status: 'paid' })
  }

  return new Response('ok')
}
```

### Checkout Page (browser)

```typescript
// app/checkout/page.tsx
import { BizupPay } from '@bizup-pay/client'

async function handleCheckout() {
  const res = await fetch('/api/checkout', {
    method: 'POST',
    body: JSON.stringify({ amount: 100, description: 'Order #1234' }),
  })
  const { pageUrl } = await res.json()

  const bizupPay = new BizupPay()
  bizupPay.mount({ pageUrl }, document.getElementById('payment')!, {
    onSuccess: () => window.location.href = '/success',
    onFailure: (err) => alert(err.message),
  })
}
```

---

## Switching Providers

The entire point of BizUp Pay is provider portability. To switch:

1. Change the import: `import '@bizup-pay/morning'` instead of `import '@bizup-pay/cardcom'`
2. Change the config: `createProvider('morning', { apiKey, apiSecret })`
3. Everything else stays identical — createSession, parseWebhook, getTransaction, refund all work the same.

You can even support multiple providers simultaneously:

```typescript
import '@bizup-pay/cardcom'
import '@bizup-pay/morning'

const cardcom = createProvider('cardcom', { ... })
const morning = createProvider('morning', { ... })

// Route by customer preference, A/B test, or business logic
const provider = customer.prefersBit ? morning : cardcom
const session = await provider.createSession(params)
```
